other_observers: some guy from Denver whose last name is Peterson or Petersen but he said that he didn't know how to ID shorebirds; I was the original finder.
specific_location: Headquarters Pond on the east side of the pond at Chico Basin Ranch.
description: This is to document a hendersoni adult Short-billed Dowitcher from Pueblo County in the Spring.  Dowitcher by long bill length.   Bill, as compared to LBDO behind this bird, is broad-based, blunt-tipped, and with a noticeable kink downwards about at the proximal 1/4 of bill.  Breast with light orange or light salmon wash. Flanks and undertail coverts with spots and only a couple bars.  Back with feather centers dark surrounded by orange that is less orange than that of LBDO back feathers. Belly slightly less orange than other ventral feathers.  Legs yellow green and longish, but noticeably shorter than the LBDO's legs. Neck light orange with only light spotting, unlike the barring on LBDO.Behaviors: This birds was in with a flock of LBDOs. The two birds in the photo slept the entire time, while the remaining LBDO flock of about 9 other birds foraged about 1 foot from the bank in shallow water.Call: characteristic call not heardPlumage: alternate
